danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures (Goombas, Koopa Troopas, etc.) with distinct sprite designs
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring avoidance or jumping to defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated environmental obstacles (lava pools, spike traps, falling blocks)
danger_signals.meaning: Instant-death or damage zones requiring precise platforming

danger_signals.visual_form: Visually distinct mushroom variants with darker coloration (made easier to distinguish in SMAS remake)
danger_signals.meaning: Lethal power-up lookalikes; hazard masquerading as benefit

danger_signals.visual_form: Large sprite of Bowser with flame-throwing animation
danger_signals.meaning: Climactic threat requiring pattern recognition and precise evasion

safety_signals.visual_form: Floating item boxes (mushroom, fire flower, invincibility star) with distinctive colors and animations
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary or permanent stat/ability enhancement; mushroom grants extra health, fire flower grants projectile attack, star grants temporary invincibility
Death penalty mitigation; increased in SMAS version for Lost Levels difficulty adjustment

safety_signals.visual_form: World progression menu; SMAS added save feature absent in originals
safety_signals.meaning: Player progress preservation; can resume from any previously accessed world

safety_signals.visual_form: Spinning yellow circular objects scattered throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Collectible resource; 100 coins grant extra life

safety_signals.visual_form: Hidden pipes or vertical vines with visual concealment

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mushroom Kingdom theming; castles, pipes, brick-block architecture

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dark browns and blacks; King Wart in SMB2 uses purples and blues
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bowser (dragon-like creature); Koopa Troopas; fire-breathing aesthetics
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Winged Koopas, fire-breathing variants, minions in different sizes and armors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dark castles, lava-filled fortresses, throne rooms; SMB2 features dream-world surrealism (Wart's domain)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Princess crown; Toad character associated as ally
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle interiors, throne room in SMB2

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Vegetables, doors, jars as interactive elements
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: King Wart and his minions; different enemy behavior in dream context
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mushroom forests, dream landscapes with unconventional architecture

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Daytime outdoor levels with blue skies, green grass, brick-block terrain; themed world variations (grass, desert, snow, sky)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard enemy patrol patterns; Mario maintains normal movement speed and jumping physics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark stone architecture, torches, lava moats; interior fortresses become increasingly ornate as difficulty escalates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy density; Bowser introduction at final castle triggers climactic battle sequence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darker color palette, brick and stone aesthetics, subterranean backgrounds; pipe networks create level connectivity
Surreal color schemes, impossible architecture, vegetable objects instead of traditional power-ups, hovering platforms, unconventional geometry
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mechanics shift: no jumping on enemies, pulling vegetables for weapons, throwing mechanics instead of stomping

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rapid color flashing overlay on Mario sprite; temporary invincibility star icon displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mario gains ability to defeat enemies via contact; visual feedback intensifies danger zone traversal safety

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mario sprite enlarges with mushroom power or displays fire-flower aesthetic; red/orange coloring for fire ability
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased health (can absorb one hit); fire flower grants projectile attack capability

environmental_storytelling.example: Level theming escalates from simple grass plains to increasingly complex fortresses and castles, visually communicating difficulty and narrative climax toward Bowser's domain
Red lava in fire-themed worlds, white/blue ice in snow worlds, sand terrain in desert worlds; visual consistency aids player environmental threat assessment
SMAS enhanced parallax scrolling and detailed backgrounds create visual depth indicating 'theme world' boundaries; each world visually distinct from previous

environmental_storytelling.technique: Narrative framing through title screens and intermissions
environmental_storytelling.example: Princess Toadstool imprisoned in Bowser's castle shown via story text; SMB2 framed as dream sequence following nightmare
Minion density and type escalate approaching fortresses; final boss encounter uses larger sprite and fire-breathing visual spectacle
Vertical vines and warp zone pipes are visually concealed but indicate world-state of 'player knowledge unlocked'; shortcut discovery rewards exploration

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walks, jumps with consistent arc physics; animation cycles communicate idle, moving, and jumping states
Reduced collision box; one-hit death state; visual smallness communicates vulnerability
Enlarged sprite; can break certain bricks; can absorb additional hit damage; growth animation communicates power gain
Throws fireballs as projectiles; sprite displays fire coloration; projectile behavior communicates offensive capability
Walks back-and-forth on platform; animation communicates mindless patrol threat
Sprite flattens when jumped on; disappears; visual state change communicates successful threat elimination
Retracts into shell when jumped; shell becomes kickable projectile; behavioral change communicates secondary threat phase
Flies between platforms; adds vertical movement dimension; flight animation communicates increased threat complexity
Large sprite; fire-breathing attack pattern; movement restricted to specific arena; behavior difficulty communicates climactic threat
Hovering animation; projectile attacks (bombs); defeat requires precise throwing mechanics; unique visual design communicates dream-world antagonist
Static sprite in throne room or castle chamber; visual presence communicates rescue objective narrative
Floating animation with up-down bob; visual distinction (mushroom shape, star spinning, fire animation); behavior communicates 'can be collected'

notes: Super Mario All-Stars is a 1993 SNES compilation of four NES-era platformers. The game communicates world-state primarily through visual-thematic variation (grass/desert/snow/castle worlds) and color-coded hazards. Danger is signaled through enemy sprites, environmental hazards (lava, pits), and boss encounters. Safety comes from power-ups (visually distinctive floating items), extra lives, and the save feature (new to SMAS). Faction identity is minimal—Mario/Luigi vs. Bowser's forces are color-coded (red/green vs. dark browns). The game lacks explicit political messaging; narrative is rescue-based (princess imprisonment). Behavioral tells rely on sprite animation and size changes (powered-up vs. normal). SMB2 notably shifts world-state communication through surreal dream-logic aesthetics and mechanic changes (pulling vegetables instead of jumping on enemies). Parallax scrolling and enhanced graphics in SMAS version improve environmental storytelling compared to NES originals. The Lost Levels difficulty was reduced in SMAS by making poison mushrooms more visually distinct and adding checkpoints.
